feature_type: security hardening / workflow enforcement (mixed fix + feat)

checks.finding: Inline decisions carry rationale: .ps1 ASCII-only for PS 5.1; id-token:write handling verified against the pinned SHA (External Integrations); guard-step placement because prompt text cannot be the trust boundary; Cross-Model-Waiver inert-by-design.
Open Questions is None with owner maintainers, Blocks Implementation: no; the two prior-open decisions are stated as fixed in requirements.md and reflected in design.md.

Each of the three Assumptions restates a spec-approved existing-system fact from requirements.md (dispatcher order, PROTECTED_GATE_SUFFIXES authority, pinned-action OIDC), the last elaborated with a concrete verification method in External Integrations; no ungrounded assumption.

## Constraint Compliance (5 rows) covers every standalone constraint (PS 5.1 ASCII, protected human-copy immutability, 1-issue-1-commit, no-prose-boundary, version-bump); regulatory 'none identified'; the two must-not edge cases are functional REQ-002/003 behaviors tracked via Test Strategy.
No numeric performance requirement; the qualitative matcher-latency note is addressed in frontend-spec Performance and restated as an accepted Medium risk.

## Deployment / CI Plan names the surface (self-improvement.yml + 3-OS test.yml), the pipeline change, and rollback; no migration; no incremental feature-flag rollout implied, so flag-strategy omission is not a gap.
## Data Plan states Data Entities: none, Existing Data Affected: none persisted, Migration Strategy: none -- the no-data-change PASS condition.

The new ## External Integrations table lists claude-code-action (pinned SHA/v1.0.165, boundary B2, OIDC contract verified against the pinned action.yml, fail-fast/fail-closed failure behaviors) and GITHUB_TOKEN (scoped by permissions, fail-closed on missing scope); no other external integration appears; CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN is the credential of the listed action, not a separate integration.
Every Components row traces to REQ-001..006; ADR Change Log states no new ADR/no boundary changes; all three User Stories map to design elements; no under/over-scoped element.

High-risk surfaces (guard write-denial, CI privilege, integration failure behavior) each name a concrete verification path (guard-r10-port/guard-cwd-bypass/claude-bash-matcher/guard-ps1-ascii tests; self-improvement-guard + empty-created-ref fixtures); no PII/fullstack journey present.
SKIP: no domain/ directory; CLI/CI hardening batch with no domain-model surface.
